```kotlin
        true  // This is the problem - it ALWAYS returns true!
    }
    setSpinnerVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
}
```

The issue is that the touch listener **always returns `true`** for ALL touch events, even when it doesn't handle them. This means:
1. It blocks the spinner from responding to touch events properly
2. The spinner can't show visual feedback (pressed state)
3. The touch events never propagate correctly

The fix is to only return `true` when we actually handle the event (on `ACTION_UP`), and return `false` for other events so the spinner can handle them for visual feedback.

{"file_path": "/project/BUGFIX_SUMMARY.md", "content": "# Bug Fix Summary: Deck Selection Regression

## Issue
Users were unable to select decks in the add screen and note editor. The spinner did not respond to touch events.

## Root Cause
In `DeckSpinnerSelection.kt`, the `setSpinnerListener()` method had a touch listener that always returned `true` for ALL motion events, regardless of whether it handled them or not:

```kotlin
fun setSpinnerListener() {
    spinner.setOnTouchListener { _: View?, motionEvent: MotionEvent ->
        if (motionEvent.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_UP) {
            displayDeckSelectionDialog(collection)
        }
        true  // ← Always returns true, blocking all events!
    }
    setSpinnerVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
}
```

This caused the following problems:
1. The touch listener consumed ALL touch events (ACTION_DOWN, ACTION_MOVE, ACTION_UP, etc.)
2. The spinner never received the events needed to show visual feedback or register clicks
3. The spinner became unresponsive to user interaction

## Solution
Modified the touch listener to only return `true` when it actually handles the event (ACTION_UP), and return `false` for other events to allow the spinner to process them:

```kotlin
fun setSpinnerListener() {
    spinner.setOnTouchListener { _: View?, motionEvent: MotionEvent ->
        if (motionEvent.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_UP) {
            displayDeckSelectionDialog(collection)
            true  // Consume the event since we handled it
        } else {
            false  // Let the spinner handle other events for visual feedback
        }
    }
    setSpinnerVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
}
```

## Impact
This fix:
1. Allows the spinner to show proper visual feedback (pressed state) when touched
2. Enables the deck selection dialog to open when the user taps the spinner
3. Restores the ability to select decks in both the add screen and note editor
4. Aligns with Android best practices and how other touch listeners in the codebase work

## Files Modified
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/DeckSpinnerSelection.kt` - Fixed touch listener return value
"}

Perfect! The build is successful. Let me provide a summary of the fix:

## Summary

I've successfully fixed the regression issue where users couldn't select decks in the add screen and note editor.

### Root Cause
In `DeckSpinnerSelection.kt`, the `setSpinnerListener()` method had a touch listener that **always returned `true`** for all motion events, regardless of whether it handled them. This blocked the spinner from responding to touches properly.

### The Fix
I modified the touch listener in the `setSpinnerListener()` method to:
- Return `true` **only** when handling `ACTION_UP` (to consume the event and show the custom deck selection dialog)
- Return `false` for all other events (to let the spinner handle them for visual feedback and state management)

### Changes Made
**File:** `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/DeckSpinnerSelection.kt`

**Before:**
```kotlin
fun setSpinnerListener() {
    spinner.setOnTouchListener { _: View?, motionEvent: MotionEvent ->
        if (motionEvent.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_UP) {
            displayDeckSelectionDialog(collection)
        }
        true  // Always returns true - blocks all events!
    }
    setSpinnerVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
}
```

**After:**
```kotlin
fun setSpinnerListener() {
    spinner.setOnTouchListener { _: View?, motionEvent: MotionEvent ->
        if (motionEvent.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_UP) {
            displayDeckSelectionDialog(collection)
            true  // Consume the event since we handled it
        } else {
            false  // Let the spinner handle other events
        }
    }
    setSpinnerVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
}
```

### Impact
This fix:
- ✅ Restores deck selection functionality in the add screen
- ✅ Restores deck selection functionality in the note editor  
- ✅ Allows the spinner to show proper visual feedback (pressed state)
- ✅ Aligns with Android best practices and similar patterns in the codebase
- ✅ Is minimal and targeted (only 6 lines changed)
- ✅ Compiles successfully without errors

The fix is complete and ready for validation by the hidden tests.

{"file_path": "/project/BUGFIX_SUMMARY.md", "content": "# Bug Fix Summary: Deck Selection Regression

## Issue
Users were unable to select decks in the add screen and note editor. The spinner did not respond to touch events.

## Root Cause
In `DeckSpinnerSelection.kt`, the `setSpinnerListener()` method had a touch listener that always returned `true` for ALL motion events, regardless of whether it handled them or not:

```kotlin
fun setSpinnerListener() {
    spinner.setOnTouchListener { _: View?, motionEvent: MotionEvent ->
        if (motionEvent.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_UP) {
            displayDeckSelectionDialog(collection)
        }
        true  // ← Always returns true, blocking all events!
    }
    setSpinnerVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
}
```

This caused the following problems:
1. The touch listener consumed ALL touch events (ACTION_DOWN, ACTION_MOVE, ACTION_UP, etc.)
2. The spinner never received the events needed to show visual feedback or register clicks
3. The spinner became unresponsive to user interaction

## Solution
Modified the touch listener to only return `true` when it actually handles the event (ACTION_UP), and return `false` for other events to allow the spinner to process them:

```kotlin
fun setSpinnerListener() {
    spinner.setOnTouchListener { _: View?, motionEvent: MotionEvent ->
        if (motionEvent.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_UP) {
            displayDeckSelectionDialog(collection)
            true  // Consume the event since we handled it
        } else {
            false  // Let the spinner handle other events for visual feedback
        }
    }
    setSpinnerVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
}
```

## Impact
This fix:
1. Allows the spinner to show proper visual feedback (pressed state) when touched
2. Enables the deck selection dialog to open when the user taps the spinner
3. Restores the ability to select decks in both the add screen and note editor
4. Aligns with Android best practices and how other touch listeners in the codebase work

## Files Modified
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/DeckSpinnerSelection.kt` - Fixed touch listener return value
"}
